steps.title: Pin down the student's goal
steps.content: The task is to “emphasize the mass of Sirius A.” The correct choice must (1) name Sirius A and (2) foreground its specific mass (2.063 solar masses). Any choice that omits Sirius A or centers on another star fails the goal immediately.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘The mass of stars, like Proxima Centauri, can be described in units called solar masses.’: Generalizes about all stars and uses Proxima Centauri as the example. Sirius A is not mentioned. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘In astronomy, the mass of stars can be described in units called solar masses, and one solar mass is roughly equal to the mass of the Sun.’: Defines the unit of measurement without mentioning Sirius A or its mass at all. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘The Sun is more massive than Proxima Centauri, which has a mass of 0.122 solar masses.’: Emphasizes Proxima Centauri’s mass, not Sirius A’s. Wrong star. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘With a mass of 2.063 solar masses, Sirius A is more massive than the Sun.’: Opens by foregrounding Sirius A’s exact mass and compares it to the Sun to make that number meaningful. Directly accomplishes the goal. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
